title: viola odorata var. sarmentosa
intro: Viola odorata var. sarmentosa, also known as the Fragrant Violets or Sweet Violets, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and fragrant oils. The plant's health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Viola odorata var. sarmentosa can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
